result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: To solve the Polyomino Mining problem, the optimal approach is to implement a Greedy Information Gain strategy. 1. Identify all possible valid placements (configurations) of the given polyominoes on the grid. 2. For each potential probe (drilling a cell), calculate the expected reduction in entropy (information gain) regarding the set of possible oil-containing cell configurations, weighted by the cost of that probe. 3. Select the probe that maximizes the ratio: (Expected Information Gain) / (Probe Cost). 4. Update the set of consistent configurations based on the query result (noisy aggregate or drilling). 5. Repeat until the confidence threshold is met or the cost of further probing exceeds the expected benefit. 6. The final set of oil-containing cells is the intersection of all remaining valid configurations. 

JUSTIFICATION: Minimizing probing cost in a noisy environment requires maximizing the information-to-cost ratio. A greedy approach using Shannon entropy or a similar metric allows for the most efficient reduction of the search space, ensuring the final guess is consistent with all query results while minimizing total expenditure.
